 {"id":"franken_networkx-uhq","title":"Remaining Utilities: graph products, k-truss, k-factor, Voronoi, SNAP aggregation","description":"## Scope
Implement ~40 miscellaneous utility functions:

### Graph Products
- `corona_product` — corona product of two graphs
- `modular_product` — modular product
- `rooted_product` — rooted product

### Structural Analysis
- `k_truss` — k-truss subgraph (maximal subgraph where every edge in at least k-2 triangles)
- `k_factor` — find k-factor (k-regular spanning subgraph)
- `k_components` — k-component structure (Moody-White decomposition)
- `onion_layers` — onion decomposition (k-core layers with additional structure)

### Voronoi & Partitioning
- `voronoi_cells` — Voronoi partition of graph based on center nodes
- `snap_aggregation` — SNAP graph aggregation (summarize graph by node grouping)

### Matching & Covering
- `min_edge_dominating_set` — minimum edge dominating set (NP-hard, approximation)
- `min_weighted_dominating_set` — minimum weighted dominating set
- `maximum_independent_set` — maximum independent set (NP-hard)
- `max_weight_clique` — maximum weight clique
- `ramsey_R2` — find Ramsey partition into clique and independent set

### Flows & Cuts
- `minimum_edge_cut` — min edge cut (might already exist, verify)
- `minimum_node_cut` — min node cut
- `stoer_wagner` — Stoer-Wagner min-cut algorithm for undirected graphs

### Chordality & Structure
- `is_chordal` — test if graph is chordal
- `chordal_graph_cliques` — cliques of chordal graph (perfect elimination ordering)
- `chordal_graph_treewidth` — treewidth of chordal graph

### Bridges & Chains
- `chain_decomposition` — decompose graph into chains (related to ear decomposition)
- `has_bridges` — quickly check if graph has any bridge

### Small-World
- `random_reference` — random graph with same degree sequence (for sigma/omega)
- `lattice_reference` — lattice graph with same degree sequence

### Miscellaneous
- `square_clustering` — local clustering variant counting squares
- `generalized_degree` — degree considering triangles
- `rich_club_coefficient` — rich-club coefficient by degree threshold
- `average_neighbor_degree` — average degree of neighbors
- `effective_size` — Burt's effective size (structural holes)
- `constraint` — Burt's constraint measure
- `local_efficiency` — average inverse shortest path of node neighborhood
- `global_efficiency` — average inverse shortest path length
- `min_edge_cover` — minimum edge cover
- `is_edge_cover` — verify edge cover
- `boundary_expansion` — vertex/edge boundary
- `node_boundary` / `edge_boundary` — boundary sets
- `dispersion` — dispersion of node pair (Facebook metric)
- `closeness_vitality` — Wiener index change on node removal

## Implementation Location
- Python: various modules under `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/`
- k_truss and k_components could benefit from Rust

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate against NetworkX for each function
- Property tests for graph products (verify product graph size = V1*V2 or similar)
- NP-hard functions: test on small instances only","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:15:02.771609031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.952958615Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.952429654Z","close_reason":"All sub-beads closed: products (69m), decomposition (3r3), utilities (64l, 2zl). Utility expansion complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","tier-4","utilities"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-uhq","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-hp3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:16:02.923936917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-ukmr","title":"parity: Graph and DiGraph edge_subgraph adj should expose mapping helpers","description":"Simple edge_subgraph views currently expose adj objects without NetworkX's mapping-style helpers. In FrankenNetworkX, hasattr(H.adj, 'items') is False and list(H.adj.keys()) / list(H.adj.values()) are unavailable, while upstream edge_subgraph views expose these helpers and return AtlasView-based values. Match the upstream adj mapping-helper contract on Graph and DiGraph edge_subgraph views.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:35:24.857274812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:35:24.857274812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-ul9o","title":"O(V²) memory exhaustion in average_shortest_path_length weighted paths","description":"In crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs, average_shortest_path_length calls fnx_algorithms::all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length which materializes the entire V x V distance matrix in memory at once. For large graphs, this causes catastrophic O(V²) memory exhaustion. The algorithm should stream results incrementally (generator/iterator) like NetworkX does, bounding memory to O(V).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:10:07.745880907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:19:21.796031390Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:19:21.794164891Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

In FrankenNetworkX, list(view.nodes.data()) raises AttributeError, while upstream NetworkX returns the expected node-data tuples. Match the upstream NodeView.data() helper contract on conversion live views.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:04:29.032916538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:04:29.032916538Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

In FrankenNetworkX, list(view.nodes.data()) raises AttributeError, while upstream NetworkX returns the expected node-data tuples. Match the upstream NodeView.data() helper contract on conversion live views.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:04:29.032916538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:35:32.017917254Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:35:32.016092520Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-ulw","title":"Spectral & Matrix: Bethe Hessian, directed Laplacian, Google matrix","description":"## Scope
Implement 7 spectral/matrix functions:
- `bethe_hessian_matrix` — Bethe Hessian matrix (deformed graph Laplacian, used in community detection)
- `bethe_hessian_spectrum` — eigenvalues of Bethe Hessian
- `directed_laplacian_matrix` — Laplacian for directed graphs (uses PageRank-based transition matrix)
- `directed_combinatorial_laplacian_matrix` — combinatorial Laplacian variant for digraphs
- `google_matrix` — Google's PageRank matrix (stochastic + damping)
- `normalized_laplacian_spectrum` — eigenvalues of normalized Laplacian
- `attr_matrix` — construct matrix from node/edge attributes

## Algorithmic Approach
- bethe_hessian_matrix: H(r) = (r^2 - 1) * I - r * A + D, where A is adjacency, D is degree diagonal, r is regularizer (default: sqrt of average degree).
- directed_laplacian_matrix: Compute stationary distribution of random walk (PageRank), form L = Phi - (Phi*P + P^T*Phi)/2.
- google_matrix: G = alpha * S + (1-alpha) * (1/N) * E, where S is stochastic adjacency.
- normalized_laplacian_spectrum: eigenvalues of D^{-1/2} L D^{-1/2}.
- attr_matrix: Build matrix from edge attribute function, rows/columns ordered by node list.

## Dependencies
- Depends on existing spectral functions in the project
- Requires scipy.sparse for efficient matrix operations

## Implementation Location
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/linalg/` — extend existing spectral module

## Test Requirements
- Verify matrix properties (symmetry, positive semidefiniteness where expected)
- Verify dimensions match graph size
- Cross-validate eigenvalues against NetworkX
- Test on known graphs with known spectra (complete graph, cycle, path)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:13:28.685600348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:41:04.703392816Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:41:04.702903159Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 spectral/matrix functions: bethe_hessian_matrix, bethe_hessian_spectrum, google_matrix, normalized_laplacian_spectrum, directed_laplacian_matrix, directed_combinatorial_laplacian_matrix, attr_matrix. Total: 455 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","spectral","tier-3"]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uo80","title":"all_pairs_lowest_common_ancestor wrapper diverges from NetworkX error contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:23:56.893768465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T02:26:30.833191467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T02:26:30.831091307Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uoxz","title":"[RW] GraphML default parity in graph attrs","description":"NetworkX stores key defaults in G.graph['node_default'/'edge_default']; current Rust parser applies defaults to node/edge attrs but does not expose defaults on graph attrs. Evaluate representation strategy for parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:54:35.662798704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:38:29.675365736Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:38:29.675110854Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -14493,10 +14493,18 @@ def __len__(self):
     def __getitem__(self, node):
         return self._view._graph.nodes[node]
 
-    def __call__(self, data=False):
-        if data:
+    def _data_rows(self, data=False, default=None):
+        if data is False:
+            return list(self)
+        if data is True:
             return [(node, self[node]) for node in self]
-        return list(self)
+        return [(node, self[node].get(data, default)) for node in self]
+
+    def __call__(self, data=False, default=None):
+        return self._data_rows(data=data, default=default)
+
+    def data(self, data=True, default=None):
+        return self._data_rows(data=data, default=default)
 
 
 class _ConversionEdgeView:

@@ -2751,6 +2751,42 @@ def test_conversion_live_views_expose_callable_degree_without_fallback(
     assert list(result.degree("missing")) == list(expected_result.degree("missing"))
 
 
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("view_fnx", "view_nx", "fnx_cls", "nx_cls", "networkx_name"),
+    [
+        (fnx.to_directed, nx.to_directed, fnx.Graph, nx.Graph, "to_directed"),
+        (fnx.to_directed, nx.to_directed, fnx.MultiGraph, nx.MultiGraph, "to_directed"),
+        (fnx.to_undirected, nx.to_undirected, fnx.DiGraph, nx.DiGraph, "to_undirected"),
+        (
+            fnx.to_undirected,
+            nx.to_undirected,
+            fnx.MultiDiGraph,
+            nx.MultiDiGraph,
+            "to_undirected",
+        ),
+    ],
+)
+def test_conversion_live_views_expose_node_data_helper_without_fallback(
+    monkeypatch, view_fnx, view_nx, fnx_cls, nx_cls, networkx_name
+):
+    graph, expected = _direction_utility_graph_pair(fnx_cls, nx_cls)
+    graph.nodes["c"].pop("color")
+    expected.nodes["c"].pop("color")
+    expected_result = view_nx(expected)
+
+    _block_networkx_utilities(monkeypatch, networkx_name)
+
+    result = view_fnx(graph)
+
+    assert list(result.nodes.data()) == list(expected_result.nodes.data())
+    assert list(result.nodes.data("color", default="missing")) == list(
+        expected_result.nodes.data("color", default="missing")
+    )
+    assert list(result.nodes(data="color", default="missing")) == list(
+        expected_result.nodes(data="color", default="missing")
+    )
+
+
 def test_reverse_helper_matches_networkx():
     digraph = fnx.MultiDiGraph()
     digraph.graph["kind"] = "digraph"
